diagnosis: The per-step survival penalty (-0.05) and fuel penalty (-0.01*(|main|+|side|)) make each timestep costly, so the agent learns to terminate early (crash) to minimize cumulative negative reward. The descent_reward is too small to offset these penalties until very close to the pad, causing the agent to never reach that state. Additionally, the leg_bonus may incentivize premature ground contact if it leads to termination without safe landing.

component_verdicts.reason: Active and positively aligned with progress, but magnitude too weak to overcome per-step penalties.
May encourage leg contact before safe landing, potentially causing early crash termination. Correlation with progress is uncertain.
Penalizes necessary thrust, creating negative incentive that discourages reaching the pad. Progress correlation is negative.
Constant negative per-step reward forces agent to seek early termination (crash) to avoid accumulating losses. It is the primary cause of early termination behavior.
Large sparse reward for safe landing is well-aligned with the task; it is not causing any issues.
